headlines: Morgan Stanley joins Bitcoin ETF race with ultra-low fee
SEC / US stablecoin & crypto rule updates and House stablecoin bill changes
Bitcoin & Ether market moves and ETF flows
CME expands crypto futures/options trading hours
CFTC/stablecoin guidance and national trust banks update (ongoing) - policy moves continuing to shape market (recent)

articles.title: Morgan Stanley prepares low-fee spot Bitcoin ETF (market-moving)
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley is moving to list a spot Bitcoin ETF with a very low management fee (reported ~0.14%), intensifying competition among Wall Street issuers and likely drawing fresh institutional flows into BTC products.
articles.url: (see Morgan Stanley S‑1 / ETF listing notices)

articles.title: SEC and CFTC signals keep shaping U.S. crypto rules (regulation)
articles.summary: U.S. regulators continue clarifying how federal securities laws apply to crypto, and co-ordination between the SEC and CFTC is increasing — a development that affects token classification, listing standards and enforcement paths.
articles.source: U.S. SEC press releases and major coverage

articles.title: House releases revised stablecoin bill with compliance changes
articles.summary: The U.S. House published a revised stablecoin bill that tightens compliance and issuer rules while including protections for developers — a key step toward formal federal stablecoin regulation.

articles.title: Bitcoin & ETH price moves tied to ETF flows and macro headlines
articles.summary: Bitcoin and Ether saw notable intraday moves after renewed ETF activity and money‑manager flows; market watchers point to spot ETF inflows/outflows as a main driver of recent volatility.
articles.source: Market trackers and crypto research notes
articles.url: (market flow reports and price charts by major data providers)

articles.title: CME Group schedules 24/7 crypto futures & options expansion
articles.summary: CME Group announced operational milestones for expanded cryptocurrency futures and options (24/7 trading windows and new connectivity dates), making institutional derivatives trading more accessible around the clock.

articles.title: CFTC guidance expands who can issue payment stablecoins
articles.summary: The CFTC reissued guidance broadening the eligible issuer types for payment stablecoins to include national trust banks, a move that could shift stablecoin market structure and institutional adoption.

articles.title: Ethereum research: new attack & network security findings (important for validators)
articles.summary: Academic and security reports revealed practical attack vectors (such as node-level eclipse attacks) against Ethereum peer‑to‑peer networking; authors disclosed findings and proposed mitigations to client teams.

articles.title: Industry adoption: major banks, wealth managers ramp up crypto product offering
articles.summary: Large financial institutions are accelerating crypto product launches — from Bitcoin ETFs to bank-level custody and token services — signaling deeper mainstream adoption and competition for client assets.
articles.source: Firm press releases and financial press reporting
